strengths: Broad distributed-systems and microservices background, including high-throughput real-time work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second.
Current hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq.
Cross-domain technical range (embedded, mobile, backend, DevOps, GIS) demonstrating adaptability across complex system boundaries.
concerns: No demonstrated engineering leadership, people management, or team-scaling experience — the entire career history is individual-contributor roles with no direct reports or organizational accountability.
No executive-level experience (roadmap ownership, board communication, governance frameworks, outsourced-engineering oversight) required by the role.
AI involvement is recent and scoped to tooling/developer infrastructure; no evidence of designing or operating production multi-agent systems with financial or regulatory consequences.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished individual contributor with solid distributed-systems fundamentals and emerging agentic-AI tooling experience, but the role demands an executive with a documented history of building and leading engineering organizations, owning architectural strategy at company scale, and operating AI in regulated fintech environments — none of which appear in his career history. The gap between his current Staff Software Engineer profile and the CTO mandate is structural, not merely a matter of missing skills.
